steps.title: Write the width in terms of $x$
steps.content:
Let $x$ be the length, in inches. The length is $26$ inches longer than the width, so:
$$\text{width} = x - 26.$$

steps.title: Apply the area formula
steps.content:
Area $=$ length $\times$ width $= 2{,}535$:
$$x(x - 26) = 2{,}535.$$
Expand: $x^{2} - 26x = 2{,}535$, then move everything to one side:
$$x^{2} - 26x - 2{,}535 = 0.$$

steps.title: Compare to each choice
steps.content:
• $0={x}^{2}-26x-2,535$: $0 = x^{2} - 26x - 2{,}535$: matches the derived equation exactly.
  • $0={x}^{2}-26x+2,535$: $0 = x^{2} - 26x + 2{,}535$: wrong sign on the constant; would mean $x(x-26) = -2{,}535$.
  • $0={x}^{2}+26x-2,535$: $0 = x^{2} + 26x - 2{,}535$: would correspond to width $= x + 26$, which contradicts "length is longer than width".
  • $0={x}^{2}+26x+2,535$: $0 = x^{2} + 26x + 2{,}535$: discriminant $26^{2} - 4(2{,}535) \lt 0$, no real solution, so no valid length.
That matches the correct answer — A is correct.
